title: Intersight Device Connector / Tunnel Health
exclusions: Excludes: PlatformType IntersightAppliance or connector hosts where ConnectionReason=MaintenanceMode is expected during CVA or PVA service windows—suppress with allow_maintenance_disconnect_pattern, maintenance scope CSV, or a temporary row in intersight_maint_resolved, not a sev-1. Single-tenant or air-gapped private-cloud pilots where the management plane is intentionally unreachable and Intersight is a future phase: do not point this alert at that inventory until a cloud path is real. Lab tenants that are permanently disconnected for curriculum or patch rehearsal. CIMC or UCSM-only estates with no Intersight claim (use classic UCS TAs). Public-cloud or partner connectors where business policy is “notify chat only, never page.” Intersight Assist VM OS disk alerts (different signal—pair with host monitoring). “Wrong account” and audit-only issues—use UC-19.1.23. Pure alarm-code triage on healthy tunnels—use UC-19.1.19. Firmware string drift while tunnels are up—use UC-19.1.20.
evidence: Saved search and dashboard: index=cisco_intersight, saved search `UC-19.1.33-DeviceReg-NotConnected` (or your standardised name) with a table of device_hostname, severity_bucket, last_change_age_minutes, and cross_validation_modtime_lag, retained per your index policy for bridge review.
dataSources: `index=cisco_intersight` and sourcetype `cisco:intersight:assetdeviceregistration` for the Intersight `asset.DeviceRegistration` managed object, polled from `GET /api/v1/asset/DeviceRegistrations` by `Splunk_TA_Cisco_Intersight` (Splunkbase 5953). Key JSON fields: `Moid`, `DeviceHostname`, `DeviceIpAddress`, `Identity`, `PlatformType` (for example `UCSFI`, `UCSX`, `HX`, `IMC`, `IWE`, `IntersightAppliance`, `Public Cloud`, `Catalyst`, `Standalone`), `ConnectionStatus` (`Connected`, `NotConnected`, `Claimed`, `Unclaimed`, `ClaimInProgress` per documented sets), `ConnectionStatusLastChangeTime`, `ConnectionReason` (for example `OK`, `MaintenanceMode`, `Disconnected`, `NoToken`, `RegistrationDisabled`), `ClaimedTime`, `ClaimedByUserName`, `AccountMoid` / `Account.Moid`, `Tags`, `Source.Moid`, `ParentSignature`. Cross-validation uses `cisco:intersight:computephysicalsummary` (`ModTime` lag). Complementary: `cisco:intersight:iamoauthtoken` for token renewal issues; `cisco:intersight:iamaccount` for CVA/PVA; optional `cisco:intersight:assetdeviceconnectormanager`, `cisco:intersight:assetdeviceconfiguration` for context panels.
app: `Splunk_TA_Cisco_Intersight` ([Splunkbase 5953](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/5953)) on-disk; marketing “Cisco Intersight Add-on” is the same product. OAuth2 API key (KeyId/Secret) with roles that can read asset and inventory: Account Administrator, Device Administrator, and Audit Administrator is a defensible read set for this UC plus the audit UC family.
spl:
// UC-19.1.33 — Intersight asset.DeviceRegistration (API /api/v1/asset/DeviceRegistrations). Sourcetype: cisco:intersight:assetdeviceregistration (Splunk_TA_Cisco_Intersight, Splunkbase 5953). Cross-validate ModTime: cisco:intersight:computephysicalsummary.
index=cisco_intersight sourcetype="cisco:intersight:assetdeviceregistration" earliest=-2h
| eval device_hostname=coalesce(DeviceHostname, device_hostname, "")
| eval platform_type=coalesce(PlatformType, platform_type, "")
| eval connection_status=coalesce(ConnectionStatus, connection_status, "")
| eval connection_reason=coalesce(ConnectionReason, connection_reason, "")
| eval last_change_str=coalesce(ConnectionStatusLastChangeTime, connection_status_last_change_time, "")
| eval lcs_epoch=if(last_change_str!="", coalesce(
    strptime(last_change_str, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.%3NZ"),
    strptime(last_change_str, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")), null())
| eval last_change_age_seconds=if(isnotnull(lcs_epoch), now()-lcs_epoch, 0)
| eval last_change_age_minutes=round(last_change_age_seconds/60, 2)
| eval reg_moid=coalesce(Moid, moid, "")
| lookup intersight_connected_devices_inventory device_hostname OUTPUT business_service, on_call_team, runbook_url, expected_uptime_pct, allow_maintenance_disconnect_pattern, warn_stale_minutes, crit_stale_minutes
| where isnotnull(device_hostname) AND device_hostname!=""
| where connection_status IN ("NotConnected", "Disconnected")
| where isnull(connection_reason) OR (connection_reason!="MaintenanceMode" AND lower(connection_reason)!="maintenancemode")
| eval allow_pat=coalesce(allow_maintenance_disconnect_pattern, "")
| where allow_pat="" OR NOT match(device_hostname, allow_pat)
| lookup intersight_maint_resolved device_hostname OUTPUT ticket_id as maint_ticket
| where isnull(maint_ticket)
| join type=left device_hostname [
  | search index=cisco_intersight sourcetype="cisco:intersight:computephysicalsummary" earliest=-2h
  | eval jn=coalesce(Name, name, "")
  | eval modt=coalesce(ModTime, modtime, "")
  | eval m_epoch=coalesce(
      strptime(modt, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.%3NZ"),
      strptime(modt, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"))
  | eval cps_lag_min=if(isnotnull(m_epoch), round((now()-m_epoch)/60, 2), null())
  | stats latest(cps_lag_min) as cross_validation_modtime_lag by jn
  | rename jn as device_hostname
  ]
| eval w_th=tonumber(coalesce(warn_stale_minutes, 5))
| eval c_th=tonumber(coalesce(crit_stale_minutes, 30))
| eval severity_bucket=case(
  last_change_age_minutes> c_th, "critical",
  last_change_age_minutes> w_th, "warning",
  1=1, "info")
| eval clower=lower(connection_reason)
| eval recommended_action=case(
  severity_bucket=="critical" AND isnotnull(cross_validation_modtime_lag) AND cross_validation_modtime_lag>30,
    "Egress, proxy, OAuth, NTP: treat as high-confidence break when inventory is also stale; open bridge and use Devices > Infrastructure in Intersight",
  severity_bucket=="critical",
    "Egress, proxy, OAuth, NTP: trace firewall, forward proxy, DNS, and device connector; compare cloud Devices view to Splunk",
  like(clower, "%notoken%"),
    "OAuth trust path: verify NTP, API key, TLS intercept, and cisco:intersight:iamoauthtoken if collected",
  1=1,
    "Transient flap vs sustained: if inventory (compute.PhysicalSummary) is fresh but registration says NotConnected, re-check Identity/Moid mapping and poll cadence before hardware swap")
| table _time, device_hostname, platform_type, connection_status, connection_reason, last_change_age_minutes, cross_validation_modtime_lag, severity_bucket, business_service, recommended_action, on_call_team, runbook_url, reg_moid
| sort 0 - last_change_age_minutes
description: Monitors Intersight `asset.DeviceRegistration` in Splunk as `cisco:intersight:assetdeviceregistration` for `ConnectionStatus` in `NotConnected` or `Disconnected` with `ConnectionReason` not in a maintenance class, `ConnectionStatusLastChangeTime`-based age, inventory lookup context, and maintenance suppression via `intersight_maint_resolved`. Left-joins `cisco:intersight:computephysicalsummary` to expose `ModTime` lag minutes as `cross_validation_modtime_lag`, and buckets `severity_bucket` with warn/crit minutes from the inventory CSV (defaults 5/30).
value: When the device connector or cloud tunnel is broken, the control plane can look quiet while the rack is not. You get an early, evidence-backed break before leadership trusts a “green” portal that is actually stale, you shorten multi-hour incidents caused by “we thought Intersight was fine,” and you give SecOps a defensible, time-stamped story next to the rest of the Splunk index so audits and hand-offs do not argue about which pane was lying.
implementation: Install Splunk_TA_Cisco_Intersight, enable `asset.DeviceRegistration` and `compute.PhysicalSummary` modular inputs to `cisco_intersight`, add props for JSON, deploy `intersight_connected_devices_inventory`, `intersight_maintenance_windows`, and the optional materialised `intersight_maint_resolved`, then schedule the `spl` every ten minutes and route actions by `severity_bucket` with 1h throttle on `device_hostname`.
visualization: Sortable table of the alert fields; single value for count of `severity_bucket=critical` in the last 6h; timechart of `NotConnected` events by `platform_type`; a companion panel of `cisco:intersight:iamoauthtoken` for OAuth; optional map of chronic oscillators by site.
dataModelAcceleration: Cisco Intersight TA does not map these JSON events into CIM by default. If you add field aliases and eventtypes so `ConnectionStatus`/`ConnectionReason` align with Network_Sessions or Alerts datasets, you may accelerate those models; keep the live alert on raw `cisco:intersight:assetdeviceregistration` until a peer review proves parity with your aliases.

references.title: Splunk CIM: Network Sessions data model

knownFalsePositives: UCSM-driven firmware or infrastructure upgrades that deliberately place the device connector into MaintenanceMode or a vendor-documented service window: route through intersight_maint_resolved, allow_maintenance_disconnect_pattern, or a change ticket, not a page. Planned reboots of an Intersight Assist virtual machine where you pre-added the hostname to a maintenance window. Controlled internet or proxy maintenance with a 2–5 minute DNS or path flap that self-heals: often stays in the info bucket; widen warn_stale if your carrier routinely flaps. Staged disconnect tests in lab tenants that you forgot to mark nonprod in the inventory. Stale DeviceRegistration with fresh ModTime: the join in this UC is Name-to-hostname; a mismatch can look like a false split—validate keys before a hardware RMA. Transient NoToken from a one-off NTP step after a daylight-saving policy push on a CIMC, corrected within minutes, should not be a critical if crit_stale is respected. This list is different from UC-19.1.19 (alarm-code churn and demo orgs), UC-19.1.20 (post-upgrade firmware lag in RunningFirmware), and UC-19.1.23 (automation that looks like a human); here the dominant “looks bad but is not” is planned maintenance, staged lab work, and chronic but below-threshold flaps—tune the CSV, not the infrastructure truth.
grandmaExplanation: We watch the long-lasting link from your data-centre equipment to the central service that centralises its health. When that link drops, fault news can stop arriving even though the machines are still there, so we help teams see the gap before they trust a quiet status screen. We also compare another timestamp so a confusing read is less likely to be mistaken for a full emergency.
hardware: Cisco Intersight SaaS control plane, Customer Virtual Appliance, and Provider Virtual Appliance integration paths; connected targets include UCS B-series behind Fabric Interconnects, UCS X-series and X-Direct, stand-alone C-series via CIMC/IMM, HyperFlex clusters, Intersight Assist on supported hypervisor builds, Intersight Workload Engine, Catalyst-attached and standalone modes where PlatformType in DeviceRegistration says so, and public-cloud or partner connector objects. The Device Connector is embedded per domain or per platform, speaks outbound HTTPS/443, heartbeats, and re-authenticates on the order of an hour, so the registration object is the right Splunk join point for `ConnectionStatus` and `ConnectionReason`.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install Splunk_TA_Cisco_Intersight from Splunkbase (Cisco Intersight Add-on, [Splunkbase 5953](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/5953)) on a Heavy Forwarder (or any node licensed for modular inputs) and on Search Heads for field discovery, lookups, and alert authoring. On-disk name must be exactly Splunk_TA_Cisco_Intersight; marketing labels such as "Cisco Intersight Add-on" refer to the same package.
• Create or use an Intersight API key pair (KeyId and Secret) in the Intersight UI under account settings, with roles that can read device asset and inventory classes: a practical combination is Account Administrator plus Device Administrator plus Audit Administrator so both asset DeviceRegistration and compute PhysicalSummary (and later audit) remain available without re-keying. Store credentials through the add-on or Splunk’s credential store; never commit secrets in inputs.conf in Git.
• Bind modular inputs to an approved index (commonly cisco_intersight) and confirm search roles include that index. Plan license and retention: DeviceRegistration is polled; volume scales with the number of registered devices and the poll interval.
• Network: outbound TCP 443 from the forwarder to the Intersight API hostname your contract lists (Cisco Intersight SaaS, CVA, or PVA depending on account type). If a forward web proxy is required, configure Splunk and OS proxy settings and validate TLS. Corporate TLS inspection must present a chain the Splunk node trusts, or the TA cannot poll and this use case is blind.
• Time: keep Search Head and indexers on accurate NTP; ConnectionStatusLastChangeTime and ModTime are evaluated with now() in SPL, so clock skew in minutes will distort last_change_age_minutes. When ConnectionReason=NoToken appears, NTP is a top suspect.
• Parsers: the TA normalises Intersight JSON into per-MO-class sourcetypes. This use case is anchored on asset DeviceRegistration, which the TA exposes as cisco:intersight:assetdeviceregistration. Cross-validation uses compute.PhysicalSummary (cisco:intersight:computephysicalsummary). Do not use fictional sourcetypes; there is no cisco:intersight:appliance in the public TA 5953 package for this signal.

Step 1 — Identify: why the Device Connector (tunnel) is the gating control plane
When the long-lived HTTPS tunnel from a Fabric Interconnect pair, a standalone CIMC platform, a HyperFlex cluster element, an Intersight Assist appliance, an Intersight Workload Engine, or a public cloud target to Cisco Intersight is broken, the operations story becomes dangerously misleading. In normal health, the embedded Device Connector on each such platform re-authenticates about hourly, heartbeats, and reports state on the order of tens of seconds. When that path fails, Intersight stops receiving new alarm transitions for those assets, inventory refresh for firmware and HCL can stall, service profile and policy reachability is impaired, and your Splunk cond/Alarms and firmware searches may show a calm chart while a rack is overheating. That is not a subtle cosmetic gap: it is a full loss of the single management-plane observability you trained the bridge to trust, while low-level ILO-style lights or in-row sensors may still show red. Distinguish three classes of problem so the right use case is engaged: (a) here — the tunnel/connector path is down or flapping, so the device is not persisting a healthy claim session to the cloud; your DeviceRegistration object shows NotConnected and a non-maintenance reason. (b) UC-19.1.19 — connectors are up and polling, but a specific cond/Alarm row is the signal you need (alarm freshness, codes, and blast radius in the alarms index). (c) UC-19.1.20 — the firmware stream is the signal when versions drift, which can happen on healthy tunnels. (d) UC-19.1.23 — the audit of who changed a policy, not whether the path is up. (e) Stale but not empty data: a partially functioning path may show Connected while inventory ModTime lags, which is why this UC also pulls compute PhysicalSummary to compute cross_validation_modtime_lag and disambiguate “registration cache oddity” from “truly no inventory motion.”
Outage cost in practice: the executive dashboard that assumes Intersight is the pane of glass for Cisco compute can silently go stale, SecOps can lose the timeline alignment between physical faults and access reviews, the SOC can stop receiving correlated hardware-adjacent context for tickets, and your change and capacity teams can approve work against yesterday’s world. Treat connector loss as a P1 on any production domain where Intersight is the system of record. If you are on CVA or PVA, the same health discipline applies, plus watch iam.Account and appliance-class PlatformType rows where AccountType is Connected or Private, because the integration path is still OAuth + HTTPS, not a second fictional wire protocol.

Step 2 — Implement: data collection, parser hints, and organisation lookups
(1) Install the TA on the polling node. In Intersight, create an API key with the roles described in Prerequisites. In Splunk, open the Intersight add-on configuration, create an account, and test connectivity to your tenant. Never paste the private secret in clear text; use the UI workflow that writes to passwords.conf. Rotate keys on the same schedule as your other platform integrations, with overlap between old and new keys to avoid a polling blackout.
(2) Enable a modular input that poll asset.DeviceRegistration at an interval that matches your alert cadence. Five minutes is a common start; a hot production estate may add a second, faster poll only after confirming against Intersight API rate documentation for your org. Reconcile the stanza name and keys to the default/inputs.conf in the exact build you run; the pattern below is representative only. Copy the real stanza from default and only override local/:

```ini
# $SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/Splunk_TA_Cisco_Intersight/local/inputs.conf
# Reconcile the stanza name with your shipped file (examples include ta_cisco_intersight://<label>).
[ta_cisco_intersight://asset_device_registration]
disabled = 0
account = intersight_prod_account
index = cisco_intersight
interval = 300
# If your TA exposes a managed object class for this input, it should reference asset.DeviceRegistration
# and the Intersight REST path family /api/v1/asset/DeviceRegistrations. Confirm exact key spellings
# (mo_class, mo_type, or equivalent) in default/ before enabling.
# endpoint = https://www.intersight.com/api/v1
```

(3) Add a second modular input (or the same input’s optional mo list, depending on build) to poll compute.PhysicalSummary so the cross-validation subsearch in the SPL has a fresh ModTime. If your TA version bundles multiple classes in a single intersight:// stanza, add compute.PhysicalSummary to that list instead of a second file; what matters operationally is that cisco:intersight:computephysicalsummary exists in the same index. Typical poll for inventory-style classes can stay at five to fifteen minutes; avoid pulling inventory every minute unless your rate budget allows.
(4) Field parsing for asset DeviceRegistration JSON. Merge into your local props or a thin wrapper app that loads after the TA:

```ini
# $SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/Splunk_TA_Cisco_Intersight/local/props.conf
[cisco:intersight:assetdeviceregistration]
KV_MODE = json
SHOULD_LINEMERGE = false
TRUNCATE = 0
# Prefer the MO’s own transition time for _time; adjust if the TA version maps a different primary timestamp.
TIME_PREFIX = "ConnectionStatusLastChangeTime":|"ModTime":|"Moid":
TIME_FORMAT = %Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.%3NZ
MAX_TIMESTAMP_LOOKAHEAD = 40
TZ = UTC
description = Intersight asset DeviceRegistration for connector/claim health
```

```ini
# $SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/Splunk_TA_Cisco_Intersight/local/props.conf
[cisco:intersight:computephysicalsummary]
KV_MODE = json
SHOULD_LINEMERGE = false
TRUNCATE = 0
TIME_PREFIX = "ModTime":
TIME_FORMAT = %Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.%3NZ
MAX_TIMESTAMP_LOOKAHEAD = 40
TZ = UTC
```

(5) Create a customer app (example id intersight_ops_governance) to hold two CSV lookups in lookups/ and the matching transforms.conf entries. In default.meta, mark them global if every role that pages should read them, or restrict to the alert owner role. Example transforms.conf:

```ini
# $SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/<your_app>/local/transforms.conf
[intersight_connected_devices_inventory]
filename = intersight_connected_devices_inventory.csv
case_sensitive_match = false
min_matches = 0

[intersight_maintenance_windows]
filename = intersight_maintenance_windows.csv
case_sensitive_match = false
min_matches = 0

# Derived, optional: pre-materialised one-row-per-host for alert suppression, rebuilt every 5–15m from the two files above; see the builder in Step 3.
[intersight_maint_resolved]
filename = intersight_maint_resolved.csv
case_sensitive_match = false
min_matches = 0
```

(6) Example intersight_connected_devices_inventory.csv. Extend the base schema with warn_stale_minutes and crit_stale_minutes so you never hardcode 5/30 in SPL. Optional allow_maintenance_disconnect_pattern is a PCRE the device may hit during image staging without paging.

```csv
device_hostname,device_ip,platform_type,business_service,on_call_team,runbook_url,expected_uptime_pct,allow_maintenance_disconnect_pattern,warn_stale_minutes,crit_stale_minutes
amersite1-fi01.example.com,10.10.1.10,UCSFI,AMER-Retail-Core,ops-ucs@example.com,https://wiki.example.com/rb/uc-19-1-33,99.9,^amersite1-fi01\\.example\\.com$,5,30
amersite1-hx01.example.com,10.10.2.20,HX,AMER-Retail-Storage,storage@example.com,https://wiki.example.com/rb/uc-19-1-33,99.5,,5,30
pdx-imm-standalone01.example.com,10.10.3.5,IMCM7,AMER-Edge-VMware,ops-edge@example.com,https://wiki.example.com/rb/uc-19-1-33,99.0,,5,30
emea-fi02.example.com,10.20.1.5,UCSFI,EMEA-Corp,ops-ucs-emea@example.com,https://wiki.example.com/rb/uc-19-1-33,99.9,,5,30
apac-xdirect01.example.com,10.30.1.8,UCSX,APAC-Analytics,ops-analytics@example.com,https://wiki.example.com/rb/uc-19-1-33,99.0,,5,30
iwe-batch01.example.com,10.30.2.9,IWE,APAC-ML-Batch,mlops@example.com,https://wiki.example.com/rb/uc-19-1-33,98.5,,5,30
cva-connector-mgmt.example.com,10.50.0.1,IntersightAppliance,GLOBAL-Platform,platform@example.com,https://wiki.example.com/rb/uc-19-1-33,99.99,,3,20
pva-partner-va.example.com,10.60.0.2,IntersightAppliance,MSP-Partner,msp-core@example.com,https://wiki.example.com/rb/uc-19-1-33,99.0,,5,30
public-cloud-conn.example.com,198.51.100.1,Public Cloud,Cloud-Brokerage,cloud@example.com,https://wiki.example.com/rb/uc-19-1-33,99.5,,5,30
lab-fi-test.example.com,10.200.0.1,UCSFI,LAB-Nonprod,lab@example.com,https://wiki.example.com/rb/uc-19-1-33,0,,30,90
dr-standalone-cimc.example.com,10.99.0.5,IMC,DR-Compute,ops-dr@example.com,https://wiki.example.com/rb/uc-19-1-33,99.0,,5,30
```

(7) Example intersight_maintenance_windows.csv. scope_filter is a PCRE against device_hostname for events inside the time window. For simple exact hosts, use ^host$ for clarity.

```csv
start_utc,end_utc,scope_filter,ticket_id
2026-05-10T22:00:00Z,2026-05-10T23:30:00Z,^amersite1-fi01\.example\.com$,CHG1234567
2026-05-12T18:00:00Z,2026-05-12T20:00:00Z,^pdx-imm-standalone01\.example\.com$,CHG1235001
2026-05-20T00:00:00Z,2026-05-20T04:00:00Z,^cva-connector-mgmt\.example\.com$,CHG1238000
2026-05-25T10:00:00Z,2026-05-25T11:00:00Z,^(emea-fi02|apac-xdirect01)\.example\.com$,CHG1240000
2026-06-01T12:00:00Z,2026-06-01T16:00:00Z,^iwe-batch01\.example\.com$,CHG1242000
```

(8) Optional but recommended: schedule a 10–15 minute saved search to materialise intersight_maint_resolved from inventory plus active maintenance windows so the alert can use a one-row-per-host file without cartesian join at search time. Builder sketch (tune to your CSV and Splunk build):

```spl
| inputlookup intersight_connected_devices_inventory
| eval mkey=1
| join type=left mkey=1 max=0 [
  | inputlookup intersight_maintenance_windows
  | eval se=coalesce(strptime(start_utc, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"), strptime(start_utc, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.%3NZ"))
  | eval ee=coalesce(strptime(end_utc, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"), strptime(end_utc, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.%3NZ"))
  | where isnotnull(se) AND isnotnull(ee) AND se<=now() AND now()<=ee
  | eval mkey=1
  | fields mkey scope_filter ticket_id
  ]
| eval in_mw=if(isnotnull(scope_filter) AND match(device_hostname, scope_filter), 1, 0)
| where in_mw=1
| table device_hostname, ticket_id
| outputlookup intersight_maint_resolved.csv
```

(9) Complementary note (different UC, but do not block on it): the health of a physical Intersight Assist virtual machine is often also visible through host metrics or SNMP to the guest OS. If the Assist is out of disk, you can see tunnel artefacts that differ from a pure “NotConnected on DeviceRegistration” story; that OS-level monitoring belongs beside this UC, not instead of it.

(10) On CVA/PVA tenants, add visibility into iam.OAuthToken (cisco:intersight:iamoauthtoken) if your subscription maps it, because failure to refresh OAuth in the long-lived path surfaces as NoToken in ConnectionReason. That stream pairs naturally with the recommended_action branch in the SPL. equipment.SwitchCard (cisco:intersight:equipmentswitchcard) can be a tertiary panel for switch-specific estates but is not required in the default alert.

Primary SPL (save as report, then clone for alert; must match the spl field in the UC JSON verbatim):

```spl
// UC-19.1.33 — Intersight asset.DeviceRegistration (API /api/v1/asset/DeviceRegistrations). Sourcetype: cisco:intersight:assetdeviceregistration (Splunk_TA_Cisco_Intersight, Splunkbase 5953). Cross-validate ModTime: cisco:intersight:computephysicalsummary.
index=cisco_intersight sourcetype="cisco:intersight:assetdeviceregistration" earliest=-2h
| eval device_hostname=coalesce(DeviceHostname, device_hostname, "")
| eval platform_type=coalesce(PlatformType, platform_type, "")
| eval connection_status=coalesce(ConnectionStatus, connection_status, "")
| eval connection_reason=coalesce(ConnectionReason, connection_reason, "")
| eval last_change_str=coalesce(ConnectionStatusLastChangeTime, connection_status_last_change_time, "")
| eval lcs_epoch=if(last_change_str!="", coalesce(
    strptime(last_change_str, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.%3NZ"),
    strptime(last_change_str, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")), null())
| eval last_change_age_seconds=if(isnotnull(lcs_epoch), now()-lcs_epoch, 0)
| eval last_change_age_minutes=round(last_change_age_seconds/60, 2)
| eval reg_moid=coalesce(Moid, moid, "")
| lookup intersight_connected_devices_inventory device_hostname OUTPUT business_service, on_call_team, runbook_url, expected_uptime_pct, allow_maintenance_disconnect_pattern, warn_stale_minutes, crit_stale_minutes
| where isnotnull(device_hostname) AND device_hostname!=""
| where connection_status IN ("NotConnected", "Disconnected")
| where isnull(connection_reason) OR (connection_reason!="MaintenanceMode" AND lower(connection_reason)!="maintenancemode")
| eval allow_pat=coalesce(allow_maintenance_disconnect_pattern, "")
| where allow_pat="" OR NOT match(device_hostname, allow_pat)
| lookup intersight_maint_resolved device_hostname OUTPUT ticket_id as maint_ticket
| where isnull(maint_ticket)
| join type=left device_hostname [
  | search index=cisco_intersight sourcetype="cisco:intersight:computephysicalsummary" earliest=-2h
  | eval jn=coalesce(Name, name, "")
  | eval modt=coalesce(ModTime, modtime, "")
  | eval m_epoch=coalesce(
      strptime(modt, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.%3NZ"),
      strptime(modt, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"))
  | eval cps_lag_min=if(isnotnull(m_epoch), round((now()-m_epoch)/60, 2), null())
  | stats latest(cps_lag_min) as cross_validation_modtime_lag by jn
  | rename jn as device_hostname
  ]
| eval w_th=tonumber(coalesce(warn_stale_minutes, 5))
| eval c_th=tonumber(coalesce(crit_stale_minutes, 30))
| eval severity_bucket=case(
  last_change_age_minutes> c_th, "critical",
  last_change_age_minutes> w_th, "warning",
  1=1, "info")
| eval clower=lower(connection_reason)
| eval recommended_action=case(
  severity_bucket=="critical" AND isnotnull(cross_validation_modtime_lag) AND cross_validation_modtime_lag>30,
    "Egress, proxy, OAuth, NTP: treat as high-confidence break when inventory is also stale; open bridge and use Devices > Infrastructure in Intersight",
  severity_bucket=="critical",
    "Egress, proxy, OAuth, NTP: trace firewall, forward proxy, DNS, and device connector; compare cloud Devices view to Splunk",
  like(clower, "%notoken%"),
    "OAuth trust path: verify NTP, API key, TLS intercept, and cisco:intersight:iamoauthtoken if collected",
  1=1,
    "Transient flap vs sustained: if inventory (compute.PhysicalSummary) is fresh but registration says NotConnected, re-check Identity/Moid mapping and poll cadence before hardware swap")
| table _time, device_hostname, platform_type, connection_status, connection_reason, last_change_age_minutes, cross_validation_modtime_lag, severity_bucket, business_service, recommended_action, on_call_team, runbook_url, reg_moid
| sort 0 - last_change_age_minutes
```

How this search behaves (pipeline walkthrough)
The search anchors on the real TA sourcetype for asset.DeviceRegistration: cisco:intersight:assetdeviceregistration. Fictional field names are avoided: the operational status is ConnectionStatus, the human-readable reason is ConnectionReason, and the last transition time is ConnectionStatusLastChangeTime, parsed to epoch with strptime. coalesce is used only for alternate spellings of the same field (camelCase vs snake_case) from different TA versions, not for arbitrary string constants. The inventory lookup adds business context and carries warn_stale_minutes and crit_stale_minutes to avoid static thresholds. Maintenance suppression uses intersight_maint_resolved, which you can generate from the scope-based CSV. ConnectionReason MaintenanceMode (case-insensitive) and optional allow_maintenance_disconnect_pattern in the inventory cover intentional work that should not page. A left join to compute PhysicalSummary, keyed on Name to device_hostname, provides cross_validation_modtime_lag in minutes; if that lag is also high, you have a stronger case that the management-plane inventory is not moving, not a transient registration glitch. The recommended_action case statement routes NoToken to OAuth, clock, and trust-chain checks. Sort order favours the longest recent disconnects first.

If join misses occur because a Fabric Interconnect’s DeviceHostname and the PhysicalSummary Name differ, extend the subsearch to include alternate keys your tenant exposes, such as Serial or a shared Identity field, after validating against one raw event of each class in the same 15m window. Never invent field names: confirm in fieldsummary first.

Step 3 — Validate: proving poll health, field truth, and suppression
(1) Modular input object over REST. On a Search Head with admin, run: | rest /servicesNS/nobody/Splunk_TA_Cisco_Intersight/data/inputs/ta_cisco_intersight splunk_server=local | table title, disabled, eai:acl.app. The exact app context may be nobody or your org’s deployer; if the path returns 404, open Settings, Data inputs, and verify a ta_cisco_intersight family input that targets asset.DeviceRegistration is enabled, then align the rest path to your build’s REST expose name. Record the last failure message from splunkd.log on the input node if disabled=1 reverts after restart: that is usually a credential, proxy, or permission error, not a Splunk search bug.
(2) Ingestion smoke. index=cisco_intersight sourcetype="cisco:intersight:assetdeviceregistration" earliest=-1h | head 5 should return small JSON with at least Moid, DeviceHostname, ConnectionStatus, and ConnectionStatusLastChangeTime when devices exist. Zero events means wrong index, no poll, or no claimed assets.
(3) Class distribution. Append | stats values(ConnectionStatus) as cs values(ConnectionReason) as cr values(PlatformType) as pt. Expect a mix of Connected and, during faults, NotConnected, with reasons such as Disconnected, NoToken, and MaintenanceMode on lab kit.
(4) Lookups. | inputlookup intersight_connected_devices_inventory and | inputlookup intersight_maintenance_windows must return headers. For the derived file, | inputlookup intersight_maint_resolved should return empty except during active windows.
(5) Controlled disconnect (non-prod). In a lab domain, add a temporary ACL block from the device connector subnet to the documented Intersight service endpoints on 443, or disable outbound NAT for a narrow test. Within one or two poll intervals, DeviceRegistration should show NotConnected with a non-MaintenanceMode reason, and the alert should class severity to critical if the state persists beyond crit_stale_minutes. Revert the ACL immediately and confirm recovery rows.
(6) Suppression proof. Insert a live maintenance window row whose scope_filter matches a host that is currently flapping, run the materialiser, confirm the host is present in intersight_maint_resolved, and verify the main SPL no longer returns that host. Remove the test row the same day.
(7) Cross-check ModTime. For one healthy server, compare ConnectionStatus=Connected in DeviceRegistration to a fresh ModTime in PhysicalSummary. For a host that you know is in trouble, a large cross_validation_modtime_lag is an amplifying signal; if lag is small while status is NotConnected, re-read raw JSON in Verbose to confirm the registration object and inventory object refer to the same device naming.
(8) Forwarder and TA CLI. On the input node, ./splunk cmd python -c "import json; print('ok')" is the wrong test; use tail -f $SPLUNK_HOME/var/log/splunk/splunkd.log | grep -i intersight in a maintenance window, and use ./splunk btool --debug to confirm local inputs override defaults. A rapid curl to the Intersight API with the same key can isolate HTTP errors without involving Splunk.
(9) Sample data check. Pick one Moid, open the Intersight API explorer or equivalent GET for asset/DeviceRegistrations, and line up DeviceHostname, ConnectionStatus, and ConnectionStatusLastChangeTime to the Splunk event at the same wall time (UTC in both places). If the cloud shows Connected and Splunk shows NotConnected, you are in the wrong org index or a stale key is pointed at a different tenant.
(10) End-to-end alert dry run. Save the search as a report, use sendalert or a dev Slack channel with throttle set long, and confirm the table columns match the runbook. Copy the reg_moid into the Intersight search bar path your tenant offers for a quick deep link during bridge.

Step 4 — Operationalize: make it live without burning out the on-call
Schedule the alert every ten minutes (cron */10) with a search window of last thirty minutes to ninety minutes of overlap depending on poll cadence, so a single delayed poll is still inside the next window. A thirty-minute lookback is a good balance with five- to ten-minute poll intervals. Map severity_bucket to action: PagerDuty or phone bridge for critical, Slack to a compute channel for warning, and a dashboard-only panel for info so transient under-five-minute stories do not wake anyone. Throttle: one alert per device_hostname per one hour in the alert definition, not only in Splunk, so reconnection storms do not open duplicate vendor tickets. Document a runbook URL in the lookup: https://wiki.example.com/runbooks/uc-19-1-33-intersight-connector and replace the domain with your wiki before production. Drilldown: the operator opens Cisco Intersight, navigates to Devices, Infrastructure, and the connection status for the device, or uses Operate and search for the host or serial, depending on the UI version your account shows. Copy the Intersight deep link that your org standardises, because exact paths change between SaaS and CVA. Pairing: if alarms look quiet, run UC-19.1.19 for alarm flow; if firmware looked golden yesterday and wrong today, run UC-19.1.20; for change correlation, use UC-19.1.23. The combination answers “is the connector the root cause, or the data plane fine and policy wrong?”

ITSI (optional). If you model Cisco compute as a service, add a health score derived from a rolling percentage of NotConnected devices weighted by business_service. Keep episodes non-paging until you are sure identity fields match, because a mis-join between DeviceRegistration and inventory will create noisy episodes. Prefer a separate KPI for “connector-healthy %” and link to this saved search as evidence.

Log retention and access: the index cisco_intersight should be readable to platform SRE and to security read-only roles that prove management-plane due diligence. Export a monthly PDF of the top ten longest disconnects for governance packs.

Runbook URL placeholder: https://wiki.example.com/runbooks/uc-19-1-33-intersight-connector (replace the hostname with your internal wiki, attach network diagrams, proxy exceptions, and Cisco TAC case categories)

Step 5 — Tune: keep the alert aligned with the real world after go-live
After two or three weeks, chart hosts that oscillate between Connected and NotConnected. Persistent oscillators are candidates for a dedicated static proxy, an MTU or path-MTU change, a flaky DNS server, or a policy-driven reconnect loop. Put those hostnames on a “chronic” dashboard with a longer warn window so change teams can work them without a nightly page. Adjust warn_stale_minutes and crit_stale_minutes per row in the inventory to reflect regional network behaviour; some regions can tolerate longer warning windows before a critical page. Revisit the meaning of NoToken: it often points to NTP skew, expired API key material on the object, or TLS inspection changes; compare against cisco:intersight:iamoauthtoken when enabled, and to server clock on the local management entity. New PlatformType values appear as Cisco ships new lines (UCS X-Direct, Intersight Workload Engine, new IMM generations); add a row to the inventory with correct team routing before the hardware lands in production. Each quarter, compare Splunk’s disconnect timeline to Intersight’s own account audit or connection event views for a sample of incidents to prove your Splunk _time and Intersight’s event stamps align within acceptable skew. Revisit the scope_filter regexes in intersight_maintenance_windows after daylight-saving changes, because a wrong UTC window will either suppress a real break or page through a change. Finally, re-evaluate the join between DeviceRegistration and PhysicalSummary if Cisco changes Name formatting; re-run a fieldsummary after each TA upgrade and extend coalesce once, not twice.

Appendix: CLI examples for the TA host (illustrative)
# Watch the modular input on Linux
cd $SPLUNK_HOME && tail -n 200 var/log/splunk/splunkd.log | grep -i cisco_intersight

# btool the effective input after a restart
./splunk btool inputs list --debug | grep -A2 ta_cisco_intersight

# Test HTTPS reachability to the documented API host (replace the hostname and path with your tenant’s values)
# curl -sS -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}\n" https://www.intersight.com/api/v1/health/Status

# Optional OpenSSL to verify chain quickly when TLS is suspected
# openssl s_client -connect www.intersight.com:443 -servername www.intersight.com < /dev/null 2>/dev/null | openssl x509 -noout -dates

This appendix is operator hygiene, not a substitute for the Splunk and Cisco documentation that govern your build.

End of implementation guide for UC-19.1.33.
